display.purpose: Screen prompts, reflective moments, and expressive empty states.

body.purpose: Controls, settings, notes, and dense product copy.

label.purpose: Section markers, metadata, and compact status labels.

shadows.name: card-ambient-light
shadows.purpose: Default card lift on light paper.

shadows.name: row-lift-light
shadows.purpose: Small repeated surfaces such as settings rows.

motion.name: fast
motion.purpose: Pressed states and tight chip feedback.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The main action for saving, logging, or confirming a deliberate user choice.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary{height:44px;padding:0 24px;border:0;border-radius:16px;background:#5B8A5B;color:#FDFCFA;font:600 15px/22px Avenir Next,system-ui,sans-serif;box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(91,138,91,.16);transition:transform 220ms cubic-bezier(.22,1,.36,1),box-shadow 220ms cubic-bezier(.22,1,.36,1),background 140ms ease-out}.ds-btn-primary:hover{transform:translateY(-1px);background:#476D47;box-shadow:0 6px 16px rgba(91,138,91,.2)}.ds-btn-primary:focus-visible{outline:3px solid rgba(91,138,91,.25);outline-offset:3px}.ds-btn-primary:active{transform:translateY(0);box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(91,138,91,.12)}

components.name: Mood Pill
components.description: A number-first mood selector element for the inverted 0-best to 10-worst scale.
components.css: .ds-mood-pill{display:grid;gap:1px;place-items:center;min-width:76px;height:52px;border:1px solid #FACFC7;border-radius:16px;background:#FDE8E4;color:#C75441;font-family:Avenir Next,system-ui,sans-serif;box-shadow:0 3px 8px rgba(199,84,65,.18);transition:transform 180ms cubic-bezier(.22,1,.36,1),border-color 180ms ease-out}.ds-mood-pill strong{font-size:20px;line-height:22px;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}.ds-mood-pill span{font-size:10px;font-weight:700;line-height:12px}.ds-mood-pill:hover{transform:translateY(-1px);border-color:#F5A899}.ds-mood-pill:focus-visible{outline:3px solid rgba(224,107,85,.24);outline-offset:3px}

components.name: Surface Card
components.description: The default warm paper container for settings, insight, and empty-state content.
components.html:
Private export

Therapist report

Prepare a clear local record only when you choose to share it.

components.name: Role Chip
components.description: Compact emotion, context, and metadata chip with role-tinted state.

components.name: Bottom Navigation
components.description: Familiar three-tab navigation for mood logging, insights, and settings.
components.css: .ds-tabbar{display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(3,1fr);gap:4px;padding:8px;border:1px solid #E5D9BF;border-radius:22px;background:#FDFCFA;box-shadow:0 5px 14px rgba(157,134,96,.08);font-family:Avenir Next,system-ui,sans-serif}.ds-tabbar a{display:grid;place-items:center;min-height:42px;border-radius:14px;color:#7A6B55;font-size:12px;font-weight:700;text-decoration:none}.ds-tabbar a.is-active{background:#E8EFE8;color:#5B8A5B}.ds-tabbar a:focus-visible{outline:3px solid rgba(91,138,91,.22);outline-offset:2px}

narrative.overview: Moodinator should feel like a personal record kept close at hand: warm paper, botanical greens, quiet structure, and clear controls that do not over-explain themselves. The product is used during small daily check-ins and sometimes during difficult moments, so the design must stay fast, readable, and emotionally unforced.
keyCharacteristics: Warm local-first surfaces rather than medical white or SaaS slate.
Clear mobile-native controls with generous touch targets.
Color carries emotional meaning, not decoration.
Calm visual hierarchy for repeated check-ins and later review.
Privacy and export flows must feel deliberate, consent-based, and reversible.

rules.name: The Emotional Semantics Rule
rules.body: Sage means stable or positive, coral means difficult or destructive, dusk means pattern and reflection, sand means privacy or neutral context. Do not swap these roles for novelty.

rules.name: The No Sterile White Rule
rules.body: Pure white and pure black are forbidden. Light surfaces stay warm; dark surfaces stay botanical.

rules.name: The Serif Has a Job Rule
rules.body: Use the display serif for screen prompts and reflective moments only. Do not use it in buttons, tab labels, settings rows, or dense data.

rules.name: The Paper Layers Rule
rules.body: Prefer tonal separation before shadow. If a surface can be separated with paper-soft, a border, or spacing, do that first.

dos: Do keep the default register product-focused: familiar controls, predictable navigation, and fast repeated use.
Do preserve local-only language in data, export, privacy, and security surfaces.
Do use Ionicons for iconography, not emoji, with the established role colors: leaf sage, heart coral, calendar dusk, lock sand.
Do make mood direction clear in text because lower numbers are better.
Do use coral carefully for difficult states without making the screen feel punitive or diagnostic.
Do use warm paper neutrals and botanical dark surfaces instead of pure white or pure black.
donts: Don't create clinical UI, hospital-like layouts, or surgical sterility.
Don't use generic AI-dashboard aesthetics, glassmorphism by default, gradient text, or decorative data cards.
Don't make the product feel forced, gamified, extractive, or like data is being harvested.
Don't use colored side-stripe borders on cards, list items, callouts, or alerts. Use full borders, icon badges, headers, or tonal backgrounds instead.
Don't call insights diagnoses or imply the app evaluates the user.
Don't change data collection, storage, or sharing without updating legal docs and in-app settings copy together.
